title: Cissus oblongolanceolata
taxonomy: Cissus oblongolanceolata is classified in the family Vitaceae. This family comprises around 12 genera of grapevines, figs, and other related plants. Cissus oblongolanceolata is part of the genus Cissus, which includes various species of climbing plants. The genus Cissus is native to tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. These plants are known for their edible, climbing habits, and diverse uses in traditional medicine.
uses: Cissus oblongolanceolata is traditionally used in African and Asian folk medicine. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Cissus oblongolanceolata, also known as Cissus quadrangularis, is a perennial climber plant native to tropical Africa and Asia, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits. The plant is valued for its potential to enhance bone density, reduce inflammation, and aid in weight loss, making it a popular natural remedy for osteoporosis, arthritis, and obesity. The bioactive constituents of Cissus oblongolanceolata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cissus oblongolanceolata is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or extract, often used to support joint health, boost bone strength, and promote overall well-being.
